title: Re-summarise a completed HB run and emit user-facing CSV exports
summary: Recompute the FitSummary from a completed HB run and write the user-facing artifacts: summary.json with segment means, mixture probabilities, log-likelihood, and per-respondent point-estimates CSV. Idempotent — safe to re-run with a different burnin.
description: Reads the persisted chain from a completed HB `fit` (or `fit-extend`) run, applies the requested burnin, and writes the user-facing artifacts: a `summary.json` containing segment means, mixture probabilities, posterior log-likelihood, and per-respondent point-estimates as a CSV. Does not sample anything — purely a post-processing step that re-derives the summary from the saved chain. Safe to re-run any number of times with different burnin values.
usage:
[["model-mnl-hb-mh::finalize"]]
run_dir = "<existing run dir>"
run_name = "hbmh_run"
# optional:
burnin = 0.2
export_dir = "<dir>"

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Directory of a completed HB fit run. Must contain `spec.json`, `manifest.postcard`, and the `chunks/` directory with the saved chain. This is what `fit` and `fit-extend` produce.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: burnin
arguments.description: Burnin fraction to discard from the saved draws. Default `0.2`. The burnin is applied at summary time only — the persisted chain is unaffected, so re-running `finalize` with a different burnin gives a different summary without re-sampling.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Where to write the CSV exports. Defaults to `{run_dir}/exports`. Override only if you want to keep multiple finalisations side-by-side (e.g., one per burnin value).

details:
`finalize` is the post-processing step that turns a raw HB chain into a stakeholder-ready summary. It reads the saved draws from `chunks/`, discards the leading `burnin` fraction, and computes:

- Segment-level mean partworths and mixture probabilities.
- Posterior log-likelihood (in-sample, and holdout if `test_input` was recorded).
- Per-respondent point estimates (the post-burnin posterior mean of each respondent's partworth vector).

The persisted chain itself is left untouched. This means `finalize` is idempotent and cheap to re-run: if you want to see how a different burnin value changes the summary, just call it again with the new value. No sampling is done, no chain state is modified.

`fit` and `fit-extend` both call `finalize` automatically at the end of a successful run with the burnin value originally supplied, so you don't normally need to invoke this command explicitly. Use it directly when you want to re-summarise an existing run with different settings.

The `export_dir` override exists so that you can keep multiple side-by-side finalisations of the same chain — for example, one with `burnin = 0.2` and one with `burnin = 0.5` — without overwriting either.
value:
On success, `export_dir` (defaulting to `{run_dir}/exports`) contains:

- `summary.json` — segment means, mixture probabilities, log-likelihood, and per-segment / overall fit statistics.
- A per-respondent point-estimates CSV — one row per respondent, one column per partworth, holding the post-burnin posterior mean.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: `finalize` does not sample anything — it only re-summarises the existing chain. To sample more iterations, use `fit-extend`.
Re-running with a different `burnin` is safe and cheap; the persisted chain is unaffected.
Use `export_dir` to keep multiple finalisations side-by-side without overwriting each other.

see_also.description: Initial HB fit — calls `finalize` automatically at the end.
Add more iterations on top of an existing chain.
Cross-validation counterpart — finalises every fold of a `cv-fit` run.
Translate the per-respondent point-estimates back into attribute / level form for reporting.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Directory of a completed HB fit run (must contain spec.json + manifest.postcard + chunks/).

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.description: Burnin fraction to discard from saved draws. Default: 0.2.
